title: Evaluation of the Federal Business Immigration Program — Appendix D: Weighting of the Survey Results - Canada.ca
content:
## Evaluation of the Federal Business Immigration Program — Appendix D: Weighting of the Survey Results
The following table presents a comparison of the distribution of the three immigration classes, the actual total population (column 2) versus the percentage of those that consented to participate (third column). The table demonstrated that the Entrepreneur class is over sampled by a factor of 2.75 (35.2% / 12.6%) while the Investor class is under sampled by a factor of 1.39 (58.2% / 82.1%).
As a Frequency of the total population (n = 12,402)
As a Percentage of the total population (n = 12,402)
As a Frequency of the survey sample (n1 = 12,364)
As a Frequency of the survey sample (n1 = 12,364)
As a Frequency of those surveyed (n2 = 850)
As a Percentage of those surveyed (n2 = 850)
Given the under-over sampling situation it was decided to weigh the results of those surveyed so that the % of each of the three classes was more proportionately representative of the total population (n1). Sensitivity analysis on different weighting options was conducted and the decision came down to three options:
Option 1: Immigration Classes
Option 2: China plus Immigration Classes
Option 3: Asia plus Immigration Classes
While the weighting exercise did bring the sample to more closely represent the population of the program, variances are still present, as depicted in the table presented on the following page.
Completed Surveys n=850
Weighted by TYPE (EN, SE, IN)
Variation in % with weighting
Weighted by TYPE and China
Variation in % with weighting
Weighted by TYPE and Asia
Variation in % with weighting
0 to 14 years of age
15 to 24 years of age
25 to 44 years of age
45 to 64 years of age
65 years of age or more
Country of last permanent residence, world area
Asia, Australia, Pacific
Non university diploma
Knowledge of official languages
Main countries of last permanent residence
People's Republic of China
United Arab Emirates
After reviewing the three options, Option 3 was selected as most closely representing the total population n1.
